# **DIGITAL QUEUE MANAGEMENT SYSTEM**

## **1\. Introduction**

The **Digital Queue Management System** is a web-based platform that helps organizations manage waiting lines efficiently. It allows customers to generate digital tokens, track queue positions in real time, and receive notifications, improving service speed and reducing crowding.  
---

**2\. Description**

The Digital Queue Management System is a full-stack web application developed using the **MERN stack (MongoDB, Express.js, React.js, and Node.js)**.

The platform allows businesses to manage customer queues digitally through a centralized system. Customers can join the queue remotely using a mobile device or kiosk and receive a digital token.

The system continuously updates the queue position and provides estimated waiting times. Notifications are sent through SMS or email when the customer’s turn is approaching.

The platform also includes an **administrative dashboard** where administrators can manage queues, assign service counters, and monitor customer flow.

Main capabilities of the system include:

 • Digital token generation  
 • Real-time queue tracking  
 • Estimated waiting time display  
 • Notification alerts  
 • Queue analytics and reporting

# **3\. Problem Statement**

Traditional queue management systems often rely on manual processes that cause several operational issues.

Some of the common problems include:

 • Long waiting lines and overcrowding  
 • Lack of visibility into queue length  
 • No estimated waiting time for customers  
 • Difficulty managing multiple service counters  
 • Inefficient customer service operations

These issues reduce customer satisfaction and create operational inefficiencies.

Therefore, a digital solution is required to automate queue management, provide real-time updates, and improve service efficiency.

---

**4\. Scenario**

1. Consider a bank where customers usually wait in long lines to access services.  
2. A customer arrives at the bank and scans a QR code or uses a kiosk to join the digital queue.  
3. The system generates a digital token and assigns a queue number.  
4. The customer can view their current queue position and estimated waiting time through the system.  
5. When the customer's turn is approaching, the system sends a notification through SMS or email.  
6. The customer proceeds to the assigned service counter when their number is called.  
7. This process reduces crowding and improves customer service efficiency.

**5\. Architecture**  
**![][image1]**

The Digital Queue Management System follows a **full-stack architecture using the MERN stack**.

### **Frontend Layer**

The frontend is developed using **React.js**.

This interface allows customers and administrators to interact with the system.

Frontend capabilities include:

 • Joining queues  
 • Viewing queue position  
 • Tracking waiting time  
 • Managing queues

### **Backend Layer**

The backend is developed using **Node.js and Express.js**.

The backend handles the application logic and API requests.

Backend responsibilities include:

 • Token generation  
 • Queue position management  
 • Notification handling  
 • Service counter management

### **Database Layer**

The system uses **MongoDB** as the database.

MongoDB stores information such as:

 • Customer queue tokens  
 • Service counter details  
 • Queue status information  
 • Queue analytics data

### **Authentication Layer**

The system uses **JWT Authentication** for secure login and role-based access.

Roles include:

 • Customers  
 • Service Agents  
 • Administrators

---

## **6\. ER Diagram (Entity Relationship)**

![][image2]

The **Digital Queue Management System** uses an Entity-Relationship (ER) model to represent how users, queue tokens, service counters, and analytics data are structured and interconnected. This model helps in designing a well-organized database and ensures efficient queue handling and tracking.

### **Main Entities in the System**

### **1\. User Entity**

Stores details of all users including customers, service agents, and administrators.

**Attributes:**  
• userId  
• name  
• email  
• password  
• role  
• createdAt

A user can generate queue tokens or manage queues based on their role.

### **2\. Queue Token Entity**

Represents digital tokens generated when customers join the queue.

**Attributes:**  
• tokenId  
• userId  
• tokenNumber  
• queuePosition  
• status (Waiting / Called / Completed)

Each token is associated with one user.

### **3\. Service Counter Entity**

Represents service points where customers are served.

**Attributes:**  
• counterId  
• counterName  
• assignedAgent  
• status

Each counter is handled by a service agent and serves multiple customers.

### **4\. Queue Analytics Entity**

Stores performance and statistical data of queues.

**Attributes:**  
• analyticsId  
• totalCustomers  
• averageServiceTime  
• queuePerformance

Used for monitoring and improving system efficiency.

## **Relationships**

• One User can generate multiple Queue Tokens  
• Each Queue Token belongs to one User

• One Service Counter serves multiple Queue Tokens

• Queue Analytics aggregates data from multiple Queue Tokens and Counters

• One Service Agent (User) is assigned to one or more Service Counters

**7\. Project Flow (System Flow)**

The system operates through the following steps:

1. Customers access the platform through a mobile device or kiosk.

2. Customers generate a digital queue token.

3. The system assigns a queue position and estimated waiting time.

4. The queue status is continuously updated in real time.

5. Notifications are sent when the customer's turn is approaching.

6. Service agents call the next customer from the queue.

---

**8\. User Flow**  
![][image3]

---

**9\. Pre-Requisites**

To build this project, knowledge of the following technologies is required.

### **Frontend**

 • React Components  
 • React Hooks  
 • React Router  
 • Axios for API calls  
 • CSS or TailwindCSS

### **Backend**

 • Node.js fundamentals  
 • Express.js routing  
 • REST API development  
 • Middleware  
 • JWT Authentication

### **Database**

 • MongoDB document structure  
 • CRUD operations  
 • Mongoose ODM  
 • Schema design

---

**10\. Required Technologies**

### **Frontend**

 • React.js  
 • TailwindCSS / Material UI  
 • Axios

### **Backend**

 • Node.js  
 • Express.js  
 • JWT Authentication  
 • Bcrypt

### **Database**

 • MongoDB  
 • Mongoose

### **Development Tools**

 • Git  
 • GitHub  
 • VS Code  
 • Postman / Thunder Client

---

**11\. Suggested Database Collections**

### **Users**

 • \_id  
 • name  
 • email  
 • password  
 • role  
 • createdAt

### **QueueTokens**

 • \_id  
 • userId  
 • tokenNumber  
 • queuePosition  
 • status

### **ServiceCounters**

 • \_id  
 • counterName  
 • assignedAgent  
 • status

### **QueueAnalytics**

 • \_id  
 • totalCustomers  
 • averageServiceTime  
 • queuePerformance

---

**12\. Key Features**

### **Digital Token System**

 • Generate digital queue tokens  
 • Assign token numbers automatically

### **Queue Tracking**

 • Real-time queue position tracking  
 • Estimated waiting time display

### **Notifications**

 • SMS alerts  
 • Email notifications

### **Admin Dashboard**

 • Manage queues and counters  
 • Monitor service agents

### **Analytics & Reports**

 • Customer flow statistics  
 • Service performance reports

---

**13\. Optional Advanced Features**

 • Mobile app integration  
 • QR code-based queue entry  
 • AI-based waiting time prediction  
 • Voice announcement system  
 • Integration with hospital or bank management systems

**14\. Learning Outcomes**

By completing this project, developers will learn:

 • Full-stack MERN application development  
 • Queue management system design  
 • Real-time system architecture  
 • REST API development  
 • Database schema design  
 • Scalable web application architecture
